title: Chewy Sweet Ethiopian Solo-Beans Wot
description: A hearty, comforting Ethiopian dish featuring tender solo-beans cooked in a rich, honey-based sauce. Serve with injera or rice for a satisfying meal.

ingredients.name: Solo-beans
ingredients.amount: 1 cup dried, soaked overnight and drained

instructions: Heat 1 tablespoon of vegetable oil in a large saucepan over medium heat.
Add the onion, garlic, and ginger and sauté until the onion is translucent, about 5 minutes.
Add the soaked and drained solo-beans and stir to combine with the onion mixture.
Add the berbere spice, tumeric, salt, and black pepper, and stir to combine.
Add the honey and stir to combine.
Add the water and bring the mixture to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low and simmer, covered, for 20 minutes, or until the sauce has thickened and the solo-beans are tender.
While the solo-beans are cooking, prepare the temper by melting the 2 tablespoons of vegetable oil in a small saucepan over low heat.
Stir in the 2 tablespoons of niter kibbeh (temper) until melted and well combined.
To serve, stir in the prepared temper and adjust the seasoning as needed.
Serve hot, garnished with chopped fresh cilantro or scallions, if desired.
